World.title: US to cut troop levels in Germany by 5,000 amid Trump spat with Merz
World.desc: The decision to reduce the US deployment to Germany comes amid a row between the two allies over Iran.

World.title: Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war
World.desc: The president writes that hostilities "have terminated" because of the ceasefire, arguing he does not need congressional authorisation.

World.title: 'If we sleep they bite': Rats and weasels infest camps for displaced Gazans
World.desc: In the Gaza Strip, the daily battles are now with rats, weasels, and other pests spreading diseases.

World.title: Spirit Airlines shutting down after rescue talks collapse
World.desc: The airline had been in talks with the Trump administration about a $500m bailout.

World.title: Trump claims other presidents flouted war powers law. It's a mixed record
World.desc: Both Bushes and Reagan won authorisation for wars, but Obama and Clinton ducked the requirement.

World.title: Australia wants to become the first country to eliminate a cancer - can it?
World.desc: For the first time, Australia has recorded no new cervical cancer diganoses in women under 25.

Technology.title: Oscars says AI actors and writing cannot win awards
Technology.desc: The academy that controls the Oscars on Friday issued new award eligibility requirements around the use of artificial intelligence in film.

Technology.title: Bright idea? UK firm pioneers data centres using lampposts
Technology.desc: The solar-powered iLamps will have a built-in Nvidia chip but there are questions over their security and scalability.

Technology.title: Pentagon says US military to be an 'AI-first' fighting force
Technology.desc: The US military has agreed eight new contracts with big tech firms as it expands its artificial intelligence capabilities.

Technology.title: Elon Musk's latest Tesla pay valued at $158bn - but he can't pocket it
Technology.desc: Musk must meet a range of ambitious milestones at Tesla to justify the monster pay packet - so far he has not.

Technology.title: Spotify adds 'Verified' badges to distinguish human artists from AI
Technology.desc: The music streaming platform will review criteria such as artists' live dates and social media presence.

Technology.title: Apple says iPhone 17 'most popular ever' as sales soar
Technology.desc: The record results come as boss Tim Cook is preparing to bow out after 15 years at the helm.

Science.title: Is this the real face of Anne Boleyn?
Science.desc: A computer science team believes they have discovered a previously unknown sketch of King Henry VIII's second wife - but not everyone is convinced.

Science.title: Is this actually what Anne Boleyn looked like?
Science.desc: An algorithm has suggested images of what were thought to be Anne Boleyn might not be.

Science.title: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
Science.desc: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.

Science.title: £20m mystery gift buys London Zoo new hospital where you can watch vets work
Science.desc: Visitors will be able to watch live veterinary procedures inside a state-of-the-art new animal hospital.

Science.title: UK's biggest ever environmental pollution claim reaches High Court
Science.desc: One of the UK's largest chicken producers and a water company accused of polluting three rivers including the River Wye

Science.title: 'We're living in a shed because of river pollution'
Science.desc: Jane and Tony Coyle spent seven years waiting for planning permission due to River Lugg pollution.
